title: France records its hottest day with relief on the horizon
subhead: With an average day-night temperature of 30°C, June 24 set a new heat record in France. Forecasters expect a gradual end to the heatwave in the western part of the country by the end of the week. High temperatures will persist until next Tuesday or Wednesday in the rest of the country.

title: New York Mayor Mamdani cements his hold on power after three of his candidates win Democratic primaries

'The special tribunal on Ukraine's aggression could be a major step toward protecting the legal architecture built after World War II'
subhead: As Russia's invasion of Ukraine has reignited the issue of the crime of aggression, international justice remains hindered by political deadlock. Claus Kress, a professor of international law, analyzes the limitations of the special tribunal established one year ago and denounces the 'double standards' undermining the system's credibility, particularly by highlighting France's position.

title: Two killed in Israeli strike in South Lebanon, Hezbollah denounces true 'violation'
subhead: Two people were killed in southern Lebanon on Wednesday in an Israeli drone strike that Hezbollah said was the latest breach of a ceasefire agreement between the two sides.

title: The unavoidable rise of a less American world
subhead: The war in the Middle East has sped up a realignment that was already underway, forcing the US's partners to rethink flexible alliances without creating overly strong dependencies.

title: 250 years of American independence: A groundbreaking Constitution for 'a more perfect union'
subhead: 'America 250' (3/13). Eleven years after the founding of the United States, there was still much left to invent. The Constitution, adopted in September 1787, offered an unprecedented balance between the three branches of government in order to achieve 'a more perfect union' of the people. The first president, George Washington, elected in 1789, remains revered to this day.

Living under Paris's zinc roofs in 40°C heat: 'One day, the people living there will look for those responsible'
subhead: As France faces extreme heat, many of these top-story spaces have become uninhabitable. Some residents have formed groups to call on their landlords, neighbors and local authorities to address their living conditions.

title: France's public pools need urgent renovation: 'What would we do without them? We wouldn't make it'
subhead: Aging, energy-intensive and expensive for rural towns to maintain, many aquatic centers in the countryside have been shutting down. Yet these facilities provide equal access for people to cool off and learn to swim. An MP recently sounded the alarm about the worrying situation facing these essential local public services.

title: France faces unprecedented heatwave: 'We are about to experience four of the hottest days ever recorded in the country'
subhead: A prolonged heatwave, rather than a brief spike, has arrived. Unusually high temperatures are set to persist across the country until at least the end of this week, exposing more than 90% of the French population to extreme and rare heat.
